presets.name: Code Review Comment
presets.template:
Write a constructive code review comment on the selected code.
Point out a concrete issue or improvement suggestion. Be specific, not vague — name the line, the symbol, or the pattern.
Avoid sounding harsh; match the tone of a respectful senior reviewer.
Preserve the user's language. Return only the comment text.

{{text}}

presets.name: Explain Code
presets.template:
Explain the selected code in 2-3 plain sentences suitable as an inline comment or short docstring.
Focus on what it does and why, not line-by-line mechanics. Preserve the user's language.
Return only the explanation text — no preamble, no code fences.

{{text}}

presets.name: Generate Test Skeleton
presets.template:
Write a unit test skeleton for the selected function or code block.
Match the language and likely test framework (e.g. XCTest for Swift, pytest for Python, Jest/Vitest for JS).
Cover the happy path and one edge case. Use placeholder values where the real input is unknown.
Return only the test code — no preamble, no markdown fences.

{{text}}

presets.name: Write Commit Message
presets.template:
Turn the selected notes or diff description into a conventional-commit-style message.
Use one short imperative subject (50 chars max), then a blank line, then a tight body if useful.
Preserve the user's intent. Return only the commit message — no preamble, no code fences.

{{text}}

presets.name: Write PR Description
presets.template:
Turn the selected commit list or notes into a pull request description.
Use this exact structure:

## Summary
- 1-3 short bullets describing what changed and why.

## Test plan
- 1-3 verifiable steps a reviewer can run.

Preserve the user's language. Return only the description — no preamble, no code fences.

{{text}}

presets.name: Postmortem Polish
presets.template:
Polish the postmortem draft below into a clear, blameless incident report.
Keep the timeline factual, separate root causes from contributing factors, and end with concrete remediation items.
Preserve the user's intent and language. Return only the polished postmortem.

{{text}}

recommendedEngines.notes: Tested DE+EN — output is structured and stays blameless.

presets.name: Friendlier Email
Short Direct Email
Brain-Dump to Structured Note
presets.template:
Restructure the selected unstructured text into a clean Markdown note.
Use level-2 headings (`## `) to group related thoughts, bullets for lists, and short paragraphs where useful.
Do not invent content. Preserve the user's language and intent. Return only the structured note.

{{text}}

presets.name: Bullets to Prose
Extract Action Items
presets.template:
Extract action items from the selected text into a checklist.
Format: each item as `- [ ] <action>` on its own line.
Each action starts with a verb. If an owner or deadline is mentioned, include it inline.
Preserve the user's language. Return only the checklist — no headings, no preamble.

{{text}}

presets.name: Prose to Bullets
presets.template:
Convert the selected prose into a tight bullet list.
Each bullet starts with a strong verb or noun, no fluff.
Preserve all facts and the user's language. Return only the bullet list.

{{text}}

presets.name: Add Edge Cases
Add Role and Goals
Improve Prompt
presets.template:
You are converting a user's rough draft into a precise prompt for an AI assistant.

A good "improved prompt" has all of these:
1. Names a fitting assistant role at the start (e.g. "You are a senior Swift engineer …").
2. States the goal in one direct sentence.
3. Lists 2-4 specific constraints, facts, or edge cases the assistant should respect. Do NOT invent constraints that aren't implied by the draft. Specifically: never pick a programming language, framework, library, or technology stack unless the user named one. If the draft doesn't suggest constraints, list at least 2 obvious failure modes the assistant should handle.
4. Names the desired output format (length, structure, tone) — but stay neutral on implementation choice.
5. Stays in the user's language — German stays German, English stays English.

If the user's draft is short, vague, or conversational, EXPAND it. Add the missing role / goal / output spec yourself based on what the user clearly wants. Do NOT just reword the draft into the same length.

Output rules:
- Return ONLY the improved prompt — one block of plain text, ready to send to an assistant.
- No preamble, no commentary, no code fences, no quotation marks around the result.
- Do not echo the user's original draft.

User's rough draft:
{{text}}

recommendedEngines.notes: Tested on small models — expanded vague inputs reliably.

presets.name: Make Prompt Negotiable
presets.template:
Rewrite the prompt below into a SINGLE new prompt that asks the AI to first push back, ask clarifying questions, or propose alternatives whenever the request is ambiguous, risky, or under-specified — and to proceed only after those questions are addressed.

The rewritten prompt must:
- Be a single new prompt, NOT the original prompt with a meta-instruction prepended.
- Preserve the user's goal verbatim in spirit (not necessarily wording).
- Stay in the user's language.
- Spell out at least one concrete trigger for clarification (e.g. deadline pressure, missing scope, ambiguous tech choice).

If the user's draft is very short or vague (e.g. "setup ci"), still produce a single rewritten prompt that names the goal and adds the clarification-first behaviour — do NOT return only a generic meta-instruction.

Return only the rewritten prompt.

{{text}}

recommendedEngines.notes: Encourages the assistant to ask clarifying questions instead of guessing.

presets.name: Polish (with Claude Code context)
presets.template:
You are improving a follow-up prompt that the user is about to send to a coding assistant.

If recent assistant replies are provided below, use them as conversational context — they describe where the conversation currently stands. Sharpen the user's prompt so it picks up that context naturally: reference concrete things from those replies when helpful, drop vague filler.

If the recent-replies block is empty or absent, do NOT invent context. Just sharpen the user's prompt on its own — make it more specific, drop filler, fix grammar.

ALWAYS preserve the user's language: German prompt → German output, English prompt → English output. Never translate. If the user's prompt mixes German and English (e.g. "und make it dark mode"), keep the dominant language.

Return only the improved prompt — no preamble, no commentary.

Recent assistant replies (oldest first, most recent last):
{{cc_recent_assistant_all}}

User prompt to improve:
{{text}}

presets.name: Structure Prompt
presets.template:
Structure the prompt below into a clean, actionable prompt.
Add sections only where useful, such as goal, context, constraints, output format, and acceptance criteria.
Preserve the user's intent and language. Return only the structured prompt.

{{text}}

presets.name: Summarize Briefly
Write CLAUDE.md Rule
presets.template:
You are converting the user's note into a properly structured CLAUDE.md feedback rule.
The note describes how the user wants Claude to behave (or stop behaving) on this project: {{cc_project_name}}.

Output requirements:
- Lead with the rule itself in clear, imperative form (one or two short sentences).
- Add a "**Why:**" line explaining the reason — often a past incident, a strong preference, or a constraint.
- Add a "**How to apply:**" line explaining when/where this guidance kicks in.
- Match the tone of typical CLAUDE.md rules: concise, specific, actionable.
- ALL three lines must be in the SAME language as the user's note. If the note is German, every line including the labels must be German — translate them to "**Warum:**" and "**Wie anwenden:**". Same for any other source language.

Preserve the user's language. Return ONLY the structured rule block, ready to paste into a CLAUDE.md file. No preamble, no commentary, no code fences.

User note:
{{text}}

presets.name: Write Memory Entry
presets.template:
You are converting the user's note into a properly structured Claude Code memory entry for project: {{cc_project_name}}.

Decide which type fits best:
- user — details about the user's role, preferences, or knowledge
- feedback — corrections or validated approaches Claude should remember
- project — facts or decisions about the current project
- reference — pointers to external systems or documentation (e.g. "bugs are tracked in Linear project X", "Grafana dashboard at URL")

CAREFUL with capitalised proper nouns: "Linear" usually refers to the project-management tool (linear.app), not to "linear/sequential" — treat as `reference` type pointing at that tool. Same for tool names like "Notion", "Slack", "GitHub", "Figma", etc.

IMPORTANT — the output MUST start with three dashes (---) on the very first line, then the YAML frontmatter, then three dashes again, then a blank line, then the body. Like this exactly:

---
name: <short kebab-or-space name>
description: <one-line description for relevance matching>
type: <one of: user | feedback | project | reference>
---

<body>

Body guidance:
- For user and reference types, keep the body short and factual (1-2 sentences). Do NOT just echo the user's note — distill it.
- For feedback and project types, structure as: rule or fact, then a "**Why:**" line and a "**How to apply:**" line.

Preserve the user's language. Return ONLY the frontmatter+body block, ready to save as a memory file. No preamble, no commentary, no code fences.

User note:
{{text}}

presets.name: Draft Comment
presets.template:
Draft a concise, natural comment or reply to the selected post.
Keep the user's language, add a useful perspective, and avoid sounding generic.
Return only the comment text.

{{text}}

presets.name: Make Thread Outline
Sharpen Social Post
presets.template:
Rewrite the selected text as a concise social media post.
Keep the language, preserve the core intent, make the hook clearer, and avoid hype.
Return only the rewritten post.

{{text}}

presets.name: Fix Grammar Only
presets.template:
Fix grammar, spelling, and obvious typos in the selected text.
Do NOT change voice, tone, vocabulary, or sentence rhythm — keep it as close to the original as possible.
Return only the corrected text.

{{text}}

presets.name: Make Formal
presets.template:
Rewrite the selected text in a more formal register.
Replace casual phrasing and contractions, but keep it natural — avoid pompous or archaic language.
Preserve all facts and the user's language. Return only the rewritten text.

{{text}}

presets.name: Make Informal
presets.template:
Rewrite the selected text in a more casual, informal tone.
Use everyday phrasing and contractions. Keep it natural — don't overdo slang.
Preserve all facts and the user's language. Return only the rewritten text.

{{text}}

presets.name: Plain Language
presets.template:
Rewrite the selected text in plain language at roughly B1 reading level.
Use short sentences and common words. Avoid jargon, idioms, and complex grammar.

Language rule (critical): keep the user's language. If the input is German, the output must be German — even slang, idioms, and casual expressions must be translated into plain German, NOT into English. Same for any other source language.

Preserve all facts. Return only the rewritten text.

{{text}}

presets.name: 5 Alternative Headlines
presets.template:
Generate 5 alternative headlines for the selected topic or headline.
Each headline on its own line, no numbering, no quotes. Vary angle and structure.
Preserve the user's language. Return only the 5 headlines — no preamble, no commentary.

{{text}}

presets.name: Hook Paragraph
presets.template:
Write a hook paragraph (3-5 sentences) for the selected topic or notes.
Open with a concrete observation, tension, or stake. No throat-clearing.
Preserve the user's language. Return only the paragraph — no headline, no commentary.

{{text}}

presets.name: Sharpen Headline
presets.template:
Sharpen the selected headline.
Make it concrete, specific, and easy to scan. Drop filler words. Avoid hype and clickbait.
Preserve the user's language and core promise. Return only the sharpened headline — one line, no quotes.

{{text}}

presets.name: TL;DR
presets.template:
Write a 1-2 sentence TL;DR of the selected text.
Capture the core point and the so-what — no filler, no hedging.
Preserve the user's language. Return only the TL;DR — no label, no preamble.

{{text}}
